hypothesis: If nudged elastic band calculations are executed on an iteratively fine-tuned, pre-trained EquiformerV2 surrogate within a Bayesian Algorithm Execution loop that acquires a small number of path points, then for LJ7 and LJ38 transitions they will reproduce classical NEB minimum-energy paths and barrier heights to the same fmax/MAEi accuracy while requiring substantially fewer expensive simulator evaluations because the surrogate rapidly learns accurate energies and forces along the transition path from sparse acquisitions.
